macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hit Record Highs Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 reached a record 7,137 mid-week before slight pullback, while Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 pushed to fresh highs despite unresolved US-Iran issues and Strait of Hormuz closure. Markets focused on strong earnings season with over 80% of S&P 500 reporters beating expectations.

items.title: Intel Earnings Crush Expectations with AI and Foundry Surge
items.summary: Intel reported Q1 revenue of $13.6B beating $12.42B estimates, with Data Center and AI unit at $5.1B (up 22% YoY) and Foundry at $5.4B (up 16% YoY); shares jumped 24% Friday. Highlights AI infrastructure investment benefits amid strong earnings start.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Weekly on Supply Worries Despite Volatile Session
items.summary: Brent crude gained 16% and WTI nearly 13% for the week amid supply concerns from US-Iran tensions, though ended mixed after Iranian Foreign Minister comments. Oil above $100 adds pressure amid Strait of Hormuz issues.

items.title: Fed Meeting and PCE Inflation Report Take Center Stage This Week
items.summary: FOMC expected to hold rates steady; focus on Chair's press conference tone amid stable labor market and inflation rising less than feared from supply shocks. Housing data like Case-Shiller shows cooling home prices, flat year-over-year.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs Above $79K Amid ETF Inflows and Sentiment Shift
items.summary: Bitcoin traded above $79,000 with a 2% gain in the past 24 hours, supported by $823.7 million in weekly spot Bitcoin ETF inflows and a shift in market sentiment from 'Fear' to 'Neutral' on the Crypto Fear & Greed Index.

items.title: Ethereum Surges Past $2,400 With 84% Volume Spike
items.summary: Ethereum traded above $2,400 with an 84% spike in 24-hour trading volumes, outperforming alongside other major cryptocurrencies including XRP and Dogecoin.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Exceed $150M in 24 Hours
items.summary: Over $111-$157 million in crypto futures liquidations occurred in the past 24 hours, with 69,691 traders liquidated globally and the largest single liquidation on Binance-ETHUSDT worth $2.4 million.

items.title: Asian Markets Rally as Global Crypto Market Cap Reaches $2.56 Trillion
items.summary: Asian equity markets moved higher with Japan's Nikkei 225 rising 1.4% to record highs and South Korea's Kospi adding 1.9%, while global cryptocurrency market capitalization spiked 1.63% to $2.56 trillion.

items.title: Banking Groups Lobby Against CLARITY Act as Trump Pushes Stablecoin Regulation
items.summary: Banking organizations are aggressively campaigning against the CLARITY Act introduction in the US Senate, while President Trump continues pushing stablecoin yield regulations.

items.title: U.S. Freezes $344M in Iran-Linked USDT Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: The Trump administration froze $344 million in USDT reportedly linked to Iran as part of ongoing pressure on Tehran amid stalled peace negotiations.

items.title: Cardano Development Lab Seeks $46.8M Budget for Bitcoin DeFi Initiative
items.summary: Cardano's founding engineering lab is requesting community approval for a $46.8 million development budget to tap into the trillion-dollar Bitcoin DeFi market.

items.title: Bitcoin Options Expiry and Oil Prices Create Volatility Setup
items.summary: Bitcoin faces $8 billion in options expiring Friday amid oil prices above $100 and a 99.5% probability of Federal Reserve rate hold, setting up potential market volatility.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: DeepSeek Releases V4 Preview Model
items.summary: DeepSeek unveiled its latest AI model, DeepSeek-V4 Preview, on April 24 as an open-weight release featuring high-performance Pro and cost-efficient Flash variants, with the Pro model boasting 1.6 trillion total parameters.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 Faces User Backlash
items.summary: Users report significant downgrades in Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 model, including poor instruction following, lazy responses, and increased hallucinations, with Anthropic acknowledging the issues.

items.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Touted as Most Intelligent Model Yet
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is described as its smartest AI model to date, with integrations like Codex powering up coding capabilities.

items.title: Kimi K2.6 Emerges as Open, Cutting-Edge AI Model
items.summary: Kimi K2.6 is highlighted as an open-source model delivering state-of-the-art performance.

items.title: Alibaba Launches Qwen3.6-27B Model
items.summary: Alibaba released its latest AI model, Qwen3.6-27B, as part of recent advancements in high-performance language models.

items.title: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang Declares AGI Achieved
items.summary: In discussions with Elon Musk, NVIDIA's Jensen Huang stated 'AGI is here,' sparking debates on economic AGI versus true general intelligence, amid xAI's Grok advancements and compute races.

items.title: Xiaomi Unveils MiMo-V2.5-Pro High-Performance Model
items.summary: Xiaomi introduced MiMo-V2.5-Pro, a high-performance AI model, alongside other Chinese firm releases like Tencent's Hy3 preview.

items.title: Google Launches Deep Research Tool
items.summary: Google introduced its new Deep Research AI tool, enhancing advanced research capabilities.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Microsoft Details 'Windows K2' Initiative to Fix Windows 11 Complaints
items.summary: Sources reveal Microsoft's ongoing 'Windows K2' project addressing user complaints about AI features, OS bloat, and performance in Windows 11. This aims to improve the operating system significantly.

items.title: Sereact Raises $110M Series B for Industrial Robot Software
items.summary: Stuttgart-based Sereact, developing software for industrial robots to handle untrained tasks, secured a $110M Series B led by Headline. The funding supports expansion in robotics automation.

items.title: Moore Threads Reports Q1 Revenue Surge to $107.89M
items.summary: Beijing-based GPU maker Moore Threads saw Q1 revenue jump 155% year-over-year to about $107.89M, achieving a $4.3M net profit versus a prior loss. This marks strong growth in semiconductors.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has built and deployed the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication. This milestone could accelerate scalable quantum computing development.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computing. This initiative scales the mobile OS to desktops for broader adoption.

items.title: Microsoft Revamps Windows Insider Program for Simplicity
items.summary: Microsoft is updating its Windows Insider Program with a simpler structure and greater user control over updates. This change responds to user feedback on update management.

items.title: Cyera to Acquire Ryft for $100M-$130M in Data Governance Deal
items.summary: Cyera has agreed to buy Israeli startup Ryft, which builds automated data access and governance tools, in a deal valued at $100M-$130M. The acquisition targets enterprise data management.
